questions: What exactly makes the 32 entries non-verifiable?
Can they be retro-hashed without touching post-anchor bytes?
Where does structured health output sit relative to the snapshot contract?

findings.summary: the 32 skips are pre-convention bodies with NO hash markup; sub-cutoff entries skip by design (ledger_hash.py:306-323, :392-405)
retro-chaining is impossible without editing post-anchor hash fields; attestation is the only chain-preserving retroactive path
attestation precedent exists: _attested_reconciled (:293-304); the migration entry strengthens it with per-entry body digests
emission is hand-assembly everywhere; ledger_emit becomes the canonical renderer, round-trip-tested against _resolve_recorded
governance_health --format json composes with the snapshot contract (ladder-level there, findings-level here)
